title: Polymer clay tassel earrings
subtitle: Teardrop earring tops with a tassel of thin polymer-clay strips beneath
excerpt: A pair of polymer clay earrings: a flat teardrop top with a small tassel of thin clay strips hanging below. The clay bakes rigid at 110°C in a domestic oven. Once cured, the earrings attach to commercial earring wires. Decorative only; polymer clay is not food-safe.
sourceNotes: Polymer clay earring construction is a contemporary studio technique with no single attributable origin. Technique derived from first principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: PVC-based modelling material that sets in a domestic oven at 110-130°C. Decorative use only; not food-safe even after curing.
The heat process that sets polymer clay in a domestic oven. The clay goes in soft and comes out rigid.

content.text: These earrings are two parts: a flat teardrop top in
polymer clay

with a small tassel of thin clay strips hanging beneath. The two pieces are joined with a jump ring after baking, so each part bakes flat for an even cure. The clay sets rigid at 110°C in a domestic oven. The finished earrings attach to commercial silver-plated or surgical-steel earring wires. Polymer clay is decorative only and not food-safe.

attrs.title: Baking polymer clay
attrs.body: Use a dedicated oven tray. Verify oven temperature with an oven thermometer before baking; many domestic ovens run hot or cold. Do not share the tray with food cookware.

items.name: Polymer clay, main colour
items.substitution: Any colour; muted earth tones read as the most wearable

items.name: Polymer clay, accent colour
items.substitution: Optional second colour for the tassel

content.text: Conditioning the clay

Squeeze and roll each colour separately in the hands for one or two minutes. The clay should soften enough that a rolled snake bends in a U without breaking. Cold straight-from-the-pack clay crumbles; conditioned clay holds detail.
Making the teardrop tops
1. Roll the main colour flat

Roll the 30 g of main-colour clay to a flat sheet 3 mm thick. Even thickness across the sheet keeps both earrings the same weight; an uneven sheet gives one heavier earring than the other and they pull asymmetric in the ear.
2. Cut two teardrops

Cut two teardrop shapes with the craft knife: 18 mm wide at the bottom, tapering to a point at the top, 25 mm tall. Use a paper template traced and laid on the clay if needed: matching freehand shapes is harder than it looks. Round the cut edges by gently smoothing them with the side of a finger.
3. Pierce the holes

Pierce two holes in each teardrop: one at the pointed top (3 mm in from the edge) for the earring wire, and one at the bottom centre (3 mm up from the edge) for the tassel jump ring. Use the needle tool pushed straight through and pulled out. Aim the holes along the centreline so the earrings hang straight in the ear.
Making the tassels
1. Roll the tassel sheet
Roll the accent colour (or more of the main colour) to a thinner sheet, 2 mm thick. Cut a rectangle 30 mm wide and 25 mm tall. Make two.
2. Cut the strips

On each rectangle, cut a series of vertical strips from the bottom edge upward to within 5 mm of the top edge. Strips around 2 mm wide give a fine tassel; 3 mm wide gives a chunkier one. Leave the top 5 mm uncut as a band that holds the strips together. The fringe falls open into a tassel as the clay relaxes.
3. Roll the band and pierce

Roll the uncut top band of each tassel into a cylinder (the strips hang down from the cylinder). Press the seam closed and smooth. Pierce a hole through the cylinder from front to back, around 3 mm in from one end, with the needle tool. This is where the jump ring attaches to the teardrop above.
Baking

Lay both teardrops and both tassels flat on a sheet of greaseproof paper on the baking tray. Lay the tassels with the strips spread out (not clumped together) so they bake straight. Check the oven temperature with the oven thermometer; aim for 110°C (fan) and verify. Bake for 25 minutes.
Curing
sets the clay rigid. Underbaked clay snaps when bent; overbaked clay browns and goes brittle.
Assembly

Let the pieces cool on the tray for ten minutes before handling. Open a jump ring with the two pliers (twist sideways rather than pulling open, which warps the ring). Thread through the tassel hole and the bottom hole of the teardrop. Close the jump ring back into a round. Open a second jump ring, thread through the top hole of the teardrop and through the earring wire loop, and close. Repeat for the second earring.
Care

Polymer clay is not food-safe. Earrings are fine for normal daytime wear. Hand cream and perfume can soften the surface over time, so spray scent and apply lotion before putting the earrings on. Wipe clean with a soft dry cloth. Store flat in a small box so the tassels do not tangle.
Variations
Stamp a pattern into the teardrop tops before baking: a small geometric stamp, a piece of lace, or a leaf vein gives texture without colour.
Cut the strips at an angle so the tassel reads as a flame shape rather than a straight fringe.
Use two contrasting colours in the same earring: cut the teardrop from one colour and the tassel from the other for a two-tone earring.

items.symptom: Tassel strips fuse together during baking
items.cause: Strips clumped on the tray; the strips melted together at the contact points
items.fix: Spread the strips out flat on the tray, with small gaps between each, before baking

items.symptom: Earrings hang asymmetrically in the ear
items.cause: Holes pierced off-centre, or the two earrings cut to slightly different sizes
items.fix: Use a paper template traced onto both pieces; pierce holes along the centreline marked with the needle tool tip before piercing fully

items.symptom: Teardrop snaps at the top hole when handled
items.cause: Underbaked, or hole too close to the edge
items.fix: Re-bake at 110°C for a further 15 minutes; on the next pair, pierce the hole at least 3 mm in from the edge

items.symptom: Strips warped or twisted during baking
items.cause: Strips not laid flat on the tray, or oven temperature uneven
items.fix: Lay the strips flat. Press the rolling pin once across the spread-out tassel to settle them. Check the oven thermometer reads a steady heat
